title: Avoid using publicly writable directories
description: Using publicly writable directories for temporary files or application data creates security risks. Attackers may be able to modify, delete, or replace files, leading to data corruption, privilege escalation, or code execution vulnerabilities.
recommendation: Use secure, application-specific directories with appropriate permissions for temporary and data files.

noncompliant.description: Using world-writable directories like /tmp, /var/tmp, or creating files with overly permissive permissions allows unauthorized access or modification.

compliant.description: Using application-specific directories with proper permissions and secure temporary file creation prevents unauthorized access to sensitive data.

checks.message: Avoid using publicly writable directories like /tmp
